faqs.a: Somatic symptom disorder is when a person has one or more real, distressing physical symptoms along with excessive thoughts, feelings, or behaviors about them. The worry and focus on the symptoms are out of proportion and disrupt daily life.

faqs.q: Are the symptoms in somatic symptom disorder real?
faqs.a: Yes, the physical experiences are real and not imagined or faked. What sets the disorder apart is the intense, ongoing distress and preoccupation that comes with the symptoms, which may or may not have a clear medical explanation.

faqs.q: How is somatic symptom disorder different from illness anxiety disorder?
faqs.a: With somatic symptom disorder, distressing physical symptoms are present and the focus is on them. Illness anxiety disorder centers more on the fear of having a serious disease, often with few or no actual symptoms.
